response: Sugar Ray gained mainstream fame in the 1990s, as evidenced by their success with the song "Fly" from their 1997 album "Floored," which led the band to shift towards a mainstream, pop music style. Against the Current, on the other hand, gained mainstream recognition in the mid-to-late 1990s, but it is not explicitly stated that they achieved the same level of mainstream fame as Sugar Ray in the 1990s.
